CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE OF SEP-12-2017 IS OFF CALENDAR. DISMISSAL ON FILE. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF WALTER THOMA'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT JULIET OLSON TO PROVIDE FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION AND PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS; AND FOR AN AWARD OF SANCTIONS. - SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING - NO APPEARANCES. THOSE WHO HAVE NOT MADE AN APPEARANCE ARE DEEMED TO HAVE STIPULATED TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR ADMISSION SET ONE NO. 15: A FURTHER SUBSTANTIVE, VERIFIED, RESPONSE IS REQUIRED FROM DEFENDANT, WHO SHALL PROVIDE SAID FURTHER, SUBSTANTIVE, VERIFIED RESPONSE TO RFA NO. 15 WITHIN 15 DAYS FROM THE DATE OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS: THE REQUESTED DOCUMENTS APPEAR RELEVANT TO THE SUBJECT MATTER OF THE LITIGATION, AND NO OBJECTIONS WERE POSED BY DEFENDANT TO THE RPD'S. ACCORDINGLY, FURTHER WRITTEN, SUBSTANTIVE, AND VERIFIED WRITTEN RESPONSES SHALL BE PROVIDED WHICH UNAMBIGUOUSLY RESPOND BY STATING, AS TO EACH PARTICULAR REQUEST, WHETHER ANY RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS ARE IN DEFENDANT'S POSSESSION OR NOT - AND AFFIRMING WHETHER ANY SUCH DOCUMENTS SHALL BE WITHHELD, AND, IF TO BE WITHHELD, ON WHAT ASSERTED BASIS. NOTHING IN THIS ORDER IS INTENDED TO PROVIDE DEFENDANT OLSEN AN EXTENSION OF TIME TO TIMELY ASSERT OBJECTIONS, WHICH APPEAR TO THE COURT TO HAVE BEEN WAIVED. SAID FURTHER WRITTEN, VERIFIED, RESPONSES, AND ACCOMPANYING PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SHALL BE SERVED NO LATER THAN 15 DAYS FROM THE DATE OF WRITTEN NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE PRO TEM: DAVID HELBRAUN, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-28-2017 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jul-17-2017 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Sep-12-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Juliet Olson To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Requests For Admission And Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents; And For An Award Of Sanctions
Off Calendar May-31-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF WALTER THOMA'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT JULIET OLSON TO PROVIDE FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION AND PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS; AND FOR AN AWARD OF SANCTIONS

No appearances. Those who have not made an appearance are deemed to have stipulated to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to Compel Further Response to Plaintiff's Request for Admission Set One No. 15: The Request, while indeed grammatically confusing, is not so poorly written as to be unintelligible, and the clear import of the Request is asking the defendant to admit that at least "one of the justifications listed in section 37.9, subdivision (a) of the S.F. Administrative Code" must be shown to justify the challenged eviction of plaintiff. The supplemental Response further objected that the Request calls for a legal conclusion. This objection was not timely raised in the initial Response and therefore is deemed waived. A further substantive, verified, Response is required from defendant, who shall provide said further, substantive, verified Response to RFA No. 15 within 15 days from the date of notice of this Order. Motion to Compel Further Responses to Requests for Production of Documents: The written responses to the subject RPD's, Set One, Nos. 1, 2-8, are not Code-compliant in that the written responses, while stating that a diligent and reasonable inquiry had been conducted, then fails to definitively state if documents responsive to the particular RPD exist in defendant's possession or not - and, if so, whether any such documents in defendant's possession will be produced or will instead be withheld on some asserted basis justifying withholding responsive documents. Here, contrary to defendant's assertion in the Opposition, the written responses to do not "fully comply" with the Code, because rather than definitively state if responsive documents exist in the responding party's possession, the response obfuscates by asserting that the responding party "is not in possession of all the documents being requested at this time" - the response does not say if any documents responsive to a particular request actually exist and will be produced, as required. The use of the words "all the documents being requested" is confusing, ambiguous, and non-responsive. Moreover, the repeated mis-identification of the responding party - defendant Olsen - as "Plaintiff" is further confusing and non compliant with the Code. The requested documents appear relevant to the subject matter of the litigation, and no objections were posed by defendant to the RPD's. Accordingly, further written, substantive, and verified written responses shall be provided which unambiguously respond by stating, as to each particular Request, whether any responsive documents are in defendant's possession or not - and affirming whether any such documents shall be withheld, and, if to be withheld, on what asserted basis. Nothing in this Order is intended to provide defendant Olsen an extension of time to timely assert objections, which appear to the Court to have been waived. Said further written, verified, responses, and accompanying production of documents, shall be served no later than 15 days from the date of written Notice of this Order. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge Pro Tem: David Helbraun, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported
